content: PM2 vs systemd for single-VPS Node.js deployment: PM2 is the pragmatic default for small/solo projects — simple CLI (pm2 start, pm2 startup, pm2 save), built-in log aggregation, auto-restart on crash, restart on boot via generated systemd unit, and can later add cluster mode. Systemd (bare unit file with Restart=on-failure) has near-zero overhead and no extra dependency but requires hand-writing the unit file and lacks PM2's log/monit convenience. For a single Node.js/Socket.io process on one VPS with no need for clustering, PM2 is the recommended choice for this project given faster setup and easier operational visibility (pm2 logs, pm2 monit) during a short-session classroom tool where quick restart/debug matters more than shaving process overhead; systemd remains a reasonable simpler alternative if minimizing dependencies is preferred.
